channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump's new energy order puts states' climate laws in the crosshairs of the Department of Justice
item.description: A new executive order from President Donald Trump that's part of his effort to invigorate energy production launches the possibility that his Department of Justice will go to court against state laws aimed at slashing planet-warming greenhouse gas pollution from fossil fuels.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump pauses tariffs on most nations for 90 days, raises taxes on Chinese imports
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125 percent.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tim Walz says Dems 'should demonize' people like Elon Musk during Ohio town hall
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz criticized Tesla CEO Elon Musk on Wednesday while talking to supporters at an Ohio town hall gathering, according to video footage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent denies that tariff pause is due to market declines
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent denied that the president's move to implement a blanket pause on his tariffs was the result of declines in the financial markets, arguing the action was part of Trump's strategy all along.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Johnson scraps vote on Trump budget blueprint in face of conservative opposition
item.description: House Republican leaders on Wednesday scrapped plans to vote on the Senate’s framework to advance key parts of President Trump’s legislative agenda, a major setback that came in the face of opposition from hard-line conservatives. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's 'buy' tip on social media before his tariffs pause made money for investors who listened
item.description: President Donald Trump offered some financial advice on Wednesday morning that ended up making a lot of money for any lucky investor who listened to him.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Options for Trump's space-based 'Golden Dome' missile defense shield head to Hegseth for approval
item.description: COLORADO SPRINGS, Colo. (AP) — U.S. Space Command said Wednesday that it has finalized options for President Donald Trump's “Golden Dome” missile defense system and has sent its recommendations to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth for his review and approval.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: Israel would be 'leader' of Iran strike if Tehran doesn't give up nuclear weapons program
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Wednesday that Israel would be the “leader” of a potential military strike against Iran if Tehran doesn’t give up its nuclear weapons program. Trump made the comments ahead of this weekend's scheduled talks involving U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden aides 'scripted' everything, allowed his faculties to 'atrophy,' new book claims
item.description: A new book claims that the White House limited former President Joe Biden's social interactions, which worsened his cognitive function.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, raises taxes on Chinese imports
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed off his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Latest: Treasury secretary says Trump will keep 10% import tariff, raise import tax on China
item.description: President Donald Trump has shared on Truth Social that he has “authorized a 90 day PAUSE, and a substantially lowered Reciprocal Tariff during this period, of 10%, also effective immediately.”.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Companies need 'time machine' for tariffs to make sense: Andrew Yang
item.description: "Instead of reshoring manufacturing, [CEOs] are just cutting investment, they're laying people off," Yang said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court lets Trump move forward with firing thousands of federal workers
item.description: The Supreme Court's stay, which allows the administration to execute the firings for now while it litigates in federal court, does not mean the terminations were lawful.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg and Christina Gatti, MPR News

item.title: New audit, renewed criticism of Met Council's oversight of Southwest light rail project
item.description: The release of a new audit of the Southwest Corridor light rail project in the Twin Cities prompted sharp questions and bipartisan criticism from Minnesota lawmakers on Monday. Officials with the Metropolitan Council said the line is on track to begin service in 2027.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Republicans rattled by tariff troubles begin to push back on Trump
item.description: Cracks are growing between congressional Republicans and President Trump over his tariff policy, with some lawmakers looking to strip him of his unilateral tariff authority and others seeking briefings from the White House on the strategy amid growing economic tumult.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens more tariffs on China as global markets plunge
item.description: President Donald Trump says he's considering additional tariffs on China. The Republican president's threat came Monday after Beijing retaliated against his announcement of tariffs last week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: RFK Jr. visits epicenter of Texas measles outbreak after death of second child
item.description: A second school-aged child in West Texas has died from a measles-related illness, a hospital spokesman confirmed Sunday, as the outbreak continues to swell.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Freedom of speech is shifting under the Trump administration. We’re exploring how
item.description: Many Americans worry freedom of speech is fading, while others feel empowered to say what they want. NPR's Morning Edition explores this dynamic in a new series, The State of the First Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel, Arezou Rezvani, Taylor Haney, and Kyle Mackie, MPR News

item.title: Here's Gov. Joe Lombardo's plan to solve the Nevada housing crisis
item.description: A $250 million plan to spur construction of homes and apartments for low- to middle-income Nevadans is projected to provide homes for 16,000 people, according to officials who presented it Wednesday night in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
